name: Basic White Sauce
recipeInstructions:
1. Combine the milk, flour, cornstarch, and salt in a saucepan or screw-top jar.
2. Whisk or shake until there are no dry bits of cornstarch or flour remaining.
3. Stir-cook for about 2 minutes, stirring until the mixture boils and thickens.
4. Add oil if desired, then stir in white pepper to taste.
Each Serving (1/4 cup): 1 Milk Choice (2%)
Serve on cooked vegetables, meat, or fish. Use as a base for crepe fillings.
Variations:
Herb Sauce: Stir in 1/2 teaspoon each of dried thyme, sweet basil, oregano, and parsley into the thickened Basic White Sauce.
Bechamel Sauce: Before adding to the thickened sauce, sauté 1 tablespoon finely chopped onion with 1 tablespoon oil when preparing Basic White Sauce. Then stir into the sauce.
Cream Salmon on Toast:
Add 2 oz. flaked salmon, 2 tablespoons frozen peas, and 1 slice of toasted whole wheat bread.
Mix the salmon with the white sauce, thaw the frozen peas in hot water, add them to the white sauce and salmon, mix well.
Toast the bread, pour the sauce mixture over it, and serve for lunch with a tossed salad.
Choices Are: 1 Milk Choice, 2 Protein Choices, 1/4 Fruit/Vegetable Choice, 1 Starch
